title: This Concord mom is making her dream come true by running Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kelly Turner, 43, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Turner, a single mom and Lyme disease survivor, finds new strength as her children grow and her health improves.
The New England Patriots Foundation supports underserved communities, aligning with Turner's goal to give back while achieving personal inspiration.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her best friend
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brette Miller is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late friend Angelle Lozano.
Lozano passed away at 29 due to a brain aneurysm; Miller aims to raise funds for The Brain Aneurysm Foundation.
Brain aneurysm rupture affects 30,000 annually in the U.S., with half not surviving these incidents.

title: Clover Food Lab puts itself on the market
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is seeking a buyer as it plans to lay off 182 workers, according to a state notice.
The fast-casual chain, with 11 locations, emerged from bankruptcy in 2024 and aims to expand in New England.
Julia Wrin Poper, CEO since 2023, succeeded founder Ayr Muir, addressing financial challenges and restructuring.

title: ‘BPS transportation is failing us’: School bus delays have Boston parents calling for change
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's school bus system serves 19,000 students, but faces delays, missed pickups, and inconsistent service affecting thousands daily.
BPS buses averaged 93% morning on-time performance, short of the 95% target, causing significant daily student lateness.
Efforts to improve include GPS tracking and hiring more drivers, but issues persist due to driver shortages and route complexity.

title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in Brian Daly's viral Seger bar video filmed at Beacon Hill's 21st Amendment bar.
Healey shared her love for Bob Seger, recalling a concert she attended while working at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom.
Daly met Healey by chance while filming; she recognized his catchphrase, leading to her participation in the video.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings, a New York-based chain, will open in Framingham, Massachusetts later this year.
The restaurant is known for its handmade soup dumplings and has received Michelin Guide recommendations annually from 2009 to 2017.
The new location will be at 84 Worcester Rd., occupying the former Fresh Market space near Shoppers World.

title: ‘The Drama’ review: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson’s darkly funny Boston movie is designed to discomfort
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Drama,' a dark comedy filmed in Boston, stars Zendaya and Robert Pattinson as an engaged couple.
Director Kristoffer Borgli explores social norms and cancel culture in this provocative and unsettling film.
The film examines the repercussions of a shocking confession made just before a wedding ceremony.

title: National UMass survey has bad news for Trump
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: UMass Amherst poll shows Trump's approval rating at 33%, lowest in his second term before 2026 midterms.
Trump's approval drops among key voter groups including men, working-class, Black voters, independents, and moderates.
Economic and foreign policy concerns, particularly Iran conflict, contribute to declining approval of Trump's leadership.

title: Globe Magazine names New England’s 50 fastest-growing companies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Boston Globe and Statista named New England's 50 fastest-growing companies despite economic challenges.
Eligible companies needed $100,000 revenue in 2021 and $1.5 million in 2024, with growth from organic methods.
Top companies include Tenovi, Salient, and HighByte, showcasing diverse sectors from AI to hospitality.

title: Judge denies Lindsay Clancy’s request for two-part murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge William Sullivan denied Lindsay Clancy's request for a two-phase trial for the alleged murder of her three children.
Clancy's lawyer sought trial bifurcation to separate issues of alleged acts and her mental state during the incident.
Clancy, accused of strangling her children in Duxbury in 2023, will stand trial on July 20 facing murder and strangulation charges.
